title: Ensure cross-origin resource sharing does not allow all  origins

input_row.title: Ensure cross-origin resource sharing does not allow all  origins
input_row.description: Cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) is a security feature that controls how applications interact with resources hosted on different domains. This recommendation applies to function apps using the Consumption, Premium, or Dedicated (App Service) plans, which support deployment slots.
input_row.rationale: Restrict CORS to only trusted origins to help enforce proper access control and reduce exposure to malicious cross-origin requests.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Service or Function App. 2. Click the name of a function app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of deployment slot. 5. Under API, click CORS. 6. Ensure Allowed Origins does not include *. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each function app and deployment slot. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list function apps: az functionapp list For each function app, run the following command to list deployment slots: az functionapp deployment slot list --resource-group -- name For each deployment slot, run the following command to get the CORS allowed origins setting: az resource show --name web --resource-group -- namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type config --parent sites//slots/ --query properties.cors.allowedOrigins Ensure that the response does not include *. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: a1a22235-dd10-4062-bd55-7d62778f41b0 - Name: 'Function app slots should not have CORS configured to allow every resource to access your apps'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Service or Function App. 2. Click the name of a function app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of deployment slot. 5. Under API, click CORS. 6. Under Allowed Origins, delete the entry that equals *. 7. Specify the origins that should be allowed to make cross-origin calls. 8. Click Save. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each function app and deployment slot requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each deployment slot requiring remediation, run the following command to update the allowed origins array to contain only the origins that should be allowed: az resource update --name web --resource-group -- namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type config --parent sites//slots/ --set properties.cors.allowedOrigins="['']"
input_row.impact: Configuration is required to ensure that the appropriate origins have access. Setting up a proper CORS policy can be fairly complex and an incorrect setting could permit Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF). The "caveat" is that if the app being deployed is a PUBLIC API, a wildcard "*" CORS policy is absolutely necessary.
input_row.default_value: By default, Allowed Origins is set to https://portal.azure.com.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_function_app_deployment_slot_cors_restriction

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use Azure CLI commands to gather necessary information about function apps and their deployment slots:
  1. **List all function apps**: 
     ```bash
     az functionapp list --query "[].{name:name, resourceGroup:resourceGroup}" -o json
     ```
     This command retrieves a list of function apps along with their respective resource groups.

  2. **List deployment slots for each function app**:
     For each function app, use:
     ```bash
     az functionapp deployment slot list --resource-group <resource_group_name> --name <function_app_name> --query "[].{name:name}" -o json
     ```

  3. **Check CORS allowed origins for each deployment slot**:
     For each deployment slot, use:
     ```bash
     az resource show --name <slot_name> --resource-group <resource_group_name> --namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type config --parent sites/<function_app_name>/slots/<slot_name> --query properties.cors.allowedOrigins -o json
     ```
     This command queries the CORS settings for the deployment slot.

- **Validation criteria**:
  - Check the result of the CORS allowed origins setting to ensure that it does not contain `"*"` which indicates that all origins are being allowed.
  - The compliance check should pass if the allowed origins array contains specific, trusted origins only, failing if `"*"` is present.

- **Expected values**:
  - A valid configuration would return a list of specific allowed origins, while a non-compliant configuration would return an array including `"*"` indicating unrestricted access.
